build_system: npm with ts-node for CDK synthesis, tsc for compilation
test_framework: Jest 30 with ts-jest, using aws-cdk-lib/assertions (Template.fromStack()), snapshot tests in test/__snapshots__/
verification_instructions: 1. Run 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx jest' to confirm all tests pass. 2. Run 'unset NODE_OPTIONS && npx cdk synth' to confirm stack synthesizes without errors.
snapshot_or_generated_files: test/__snapshots__/cdk-base.test.ts.snap - regenerated by running 'npx jest --updateSnapshot'. The snapshot will change when new resources are added to the stack.
environment_constraints: COMMON_DEPENDENCIES network mode - npm registry accessible. Node.js v22 available. No Docker daemon needed (CDK synth runs locally). Must unset NODE_OPTIONS before running jest/cdk commands.
contribution_requirements: Strict TDD: write failing tests first, then minimal implementation. Conventional Commits required. ARCHITECTURE.md must be updated whenever AWS resources change. Prefer L2/L3 constructs. Follow least-privilege IAM and encryption best practices.
key_patterns: Stack defined in lib/cdk-base-stack.ts extending cdk.Stack. Tests in test/cdk-base.test.ts using Template.fromStack(). App entry in bin/cdk-base.ts. jest.config.js uses setupFilesAfterEnv with aws-cdk-lib/testhelpers/jest-autoclean. Empty stack produces only Parameters and Rules keys (no Resources).
directory_structure: bin/ - CDK app entry point. lib/ - Stack definitions. test/ - Jest test files with __snapshots__/ subdirectory. Root has config files (cdk.json, tsconfig.json, jest.config.js, package.json).
